title: clinopodium alpinum subsp. hungaricum
intro: Clinopodium alpinum subsp. hungaricum, also known as Hungarian clinopodium, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Central and Eastern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Hungarian clinopodium include its ability to relieve digestive issues, alleviate respiratory problems, and reduce menstrual cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, Hungarian clinopodium can be used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
